header.title: Character Counter — Count Characters, Words & Lines for SEO
header.description: Free online character counter for SEO to count characters, words, lines, and paragraphs in real-time. Perfect for optimizing meta descriptions, title tags, and social media posts to stay within length limits. Works entirely in your browser with no data uploads.
header.keywords: character counter, seo character counter, count characters online, meta description counter, title tag counter, word counter seo

hints.seo-title: Google truncates titles over ~60 chars. Keep primary keyword near the start.
hints.seo-description: Google truncates around 155–160 chars on desktop. Use the full limit.
hints.og-title: Open Graph title used by Facebook & LinkedIn. ~95 chars before truncation.
hints.og-description: Open Graph description. Facebook shows ~200 chars.
hints.tweet: Twitter/X limits posts to 280 characters. URLs count as 23 chars.
hints.facebook: Optimal length is under 80 chars for max engagement. Facebook shows 'See more' after ~280 chars on mobile.
hints.linkedin-post: LinkedIn shows 'See more' after ~210 chars on mobile, ~700 on desktop. Ideal: 150–300 chars.
hints.linkedin-headline: LinkedIn profile headline has a 220 char limit. Around 120 chars shows without truncation in search.
hints.instagram: Instagram caps at 2200 chars but 'More' appears after ~125 chars. Keep captions punchy.
hints.email-subject: Most email clients show 40–60 chars. Mobile shows even fewer (~30). Keep it under 50.

items.question: Why are character counts important for SEO?
items.answer: Search engines truncate meta titles over 60 characters and meta descriptions over 160 characters. Keeping within limits ensures your snippets display fully in search results.

items.question: Does word count affect SEO ranking?
items.answer: While word count alone is not a direct ranking factor, comprehensive content that covers a topic in depth tends to perform better in search results.

items.question: What is keyword density?
items.answer: Keyword density is the percentage of times a keyword appears in your content compared to the total word count. A natural density of 1-3% is generally recommended.

items.question: Are spaces counted as characters?
items.answer: Yes, most character counters include spaces in the total character count. Meta descriptions typically have a 160-character limit including spaces.
